questions.q: Whose son was Bindusara?
questions.explanation: Bindusara was the son of Chandragupta Maurya, founder of the Mauryan Empire.

questions.q: To which dynasty did Bindusara belong?
questions.explanation: Bindusara was the second ruler of the Maurya dynasty.

questions.q: In which century did Bindusara rule the Mauryan Empire?
questions.explanation: Bindusara ruled the Mauryan Empire in the early 3rd century BCE.

questions.q: By what name did Greek sources refer to Bindusara?
questions.explanation: Greek sources call Bindusara 'Amitrochates'.

questions.q: Who succeeded Bindusara as Mauryan emperor?
questions.explanation: Bindusara was succeeded by his son Ashoka, the third Mauryan emperor.

questions.q: What did Bindusara mainly do with the vast empire he inherited?
options: Reduced it to a single city
questions.explanation: Bindusara maintained the vast extent of the empire built by his father.

questions.q: Bindusara kept up diplomatic contact with kingdoms in which direction?
questions.explanation: Bindusara maintained diplomatic contact with the Hellenistic kingdoms to the west.

questions.q: The Hellenistic kingdoms Bindusara dealt with were successors of which conqueror?
questions.explanation: The Hellenistic kingdoms to the west were founded by the successors of Alexander the Great.

questions.q: Bindusara's father had founded the empire by overthrowing which dynasty?
questions.explanation: Bindusara's father Chandragupta overthrew the Nanda dynasty to found the Maurya empire.

questions.q: From which capital did Bindusara rule?
questions.explanation: Like the other Mauryan emperors, Bindusara ruled from Pataliputra.

questions.q: The Greek title 'Amitrochates' is generally taken to render which Sanskrit epithet?
questions.explanation: Amitrochates renders 'Amitraghata', meaning slayer of enemies, the epithet noted in this card's subtitle.

questions.q: According to tradition, which Seleucid king sent an envoy named Deimachus to Bindusara's court?
questions.explanation: Antiochus I Soter is said to have sent Deimachus as ambassador to Bindusara.

questions.q: A famous anecdote says Bindusara requested wine, figs, and what else from a Greek king?
questions.explanation: Tradition says Bindusara asked Antiochus for sweet wine, dried figs, and a sophist, the last being politely refused.

questions.q: Which religious sect did Bindusara reportedly favour, according to tradition?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Bindusara was a patron of the Ajivika sect.

questions.q: Roughly how long is Bindusara generally believed to have reigned?
questions.explanation: Bindusara is generally credited with a reign of about 25 years in the early 3rd century BCE.

questions.q: Which strategist of his father's reign is said by tradition to have continued serving Bindusara as a minister?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Chanakya, Chandragupta's mentor, continued as a minister under Bindusara.

questions.q: Before becoming emperor, Ashoka served Bindusara as a governor; at which northwestern city was he posted in tradition?
questions.explanation: Ashoka is said to have served as a Mauryan governor, including at Taxila, during his father Bindusara's reign.

questions.q: Bindusara is said to have suppressed a revolt in which important Mauryan province during his reign?
questions.explanation: Tradition records a rebellion at Taxila during Bindusara's reign, which his sons were sent to subdue.

questions.q: Bindusara stands chronologically between which two famous Mauryan emperors?
questions.explanation: Bindusara reigned between his father Chandragupta and his son Ashoka.

questions.q: Which region of the far south is sometimes said to have been left unconquered until late in Mauryan times, suggesting limits to Bindusara's expansion?
options: The extreme Tamil country (Cholas, Pandyas, Cheras)
questions.explanation: The southernmost Tamil kingdoms remained largely outside Mauryan control, marking the limits of the empire under Bindusara.

questions.q: Bindusara's diplomatic exchanges show the Mauryan Empire had standing relations with which broad cultural world?
questions.explanation: Bindusara exchanged embassies with the Hellenistic Greek successor kingdoms of Alexander.

questions.q: Which envoy did Ptolemy II of Egypt reportedly send to the Mauryan court in this era?
questions.explanation: Ptolemy II Philadelphus of Egypt is said to have sent an envoy named Dionysius to the Mauryan court.

questions.q: Which northern Buddhist tradition names Bindusara's chief queen and Ashoka's mother as Subhadrangi (or Dharma)?
questions.explanation: The Ashokavadana, part of the Divyavadana, names Ashoka's mother and recounts Bindusara's family.

questions.q: Which minister, according to the Ashokavadana, helped Ashoka secure the throne after Bindusara's death?
questions.explanation: The Ashokavadana credits the minister Radhagupta with helping Ashoka gain the throne after Bindusara died.

questions.q: Jain tradition (Hemachandra's Parishishtaparvan) gives Bindusara which alternative name?
questions.explanation: Jain tradition in the Parishishtaparvan refers to Bindusara as Simhasena.

questions.q: A late legend explains Bindusara's name through a drop of which substance reportedly linked to his birth?
options: A drop of poison (bindu) mixed in food
A drop of blood from a battle
questions.explanation: A legend connects 'Bindu' to a drop of poison Chanakya had been mixing into Chandragupta's food, marking the infant.

questions.q: The Sri Lankan chronicle the Mahavamsa records that Bindusara had how many sons in its tradition?
questions.explanation: The Mahavamsa tradition holds that Bindusara had a large number of sons, famously cited as one hundred, of whom Ashoka prevailed.

questions.q: Bindusara's reign is poorly documented because, unlike his son, he left behind little of which kind of evidence?
questions.explanation: Unlike Ashoka's many edicts, Bindusara left almost no inscriptions, so his reign is sparsely documented.

questions.q: The philosopher Bindusara requested from the West was refused because Greek law reportedly forbade what?
options: The sale or export of a sophist (free philosopher)
questions.explanation: Antiochus reportedly replied that Greek law did not allow a sophist to be sold or sent like merchandise.

questions.q: Bindusara's epithet 'slayer of enemies' has led some historians to suggest he extended Mauryan control over which region of India?
options: Central Asia beyond the Hindu Kush
questions.explanation: His epithet Amitraghata has led some scholars to credit Bindusara with consolidating Mauryan power in the Deccan and peninsular India.
